events.title: Orchard Hill Habitat Restoration Volunteer Day
events.description: The UMass Bee Campus Committee needs your help making pollinator habitat on Orchard Hill! Please join us for our second invasive species pull day. This will be a fun chance to learn how to identify invasive plants on campus and help us prepare a site for pollinator habitat restoration! We will end the day with a short pollinator tour on Orchard Hill. What to bring: Wear comfortable clothes, long pants, and closed toed shoes with a good tread that you don't mind getting dirty pulling invasive plants. events.title: Memory, Archives, and Commemoration: Remembering the Soweto Uprising of 16 June, 1976
events.venue: Library, W. E. B. Du Bois
events.description: Livestream link here. Much of our work as librarians, as library staff, as archivists, and as educators, is to work to hold and store memory. We work to facilitate a remembering, whether our own or that of another place or time. Our work as people extends beyond that too; we are storytellers. Some of us carry these stories in our minds, some in our art - poetry, fiction, photographs, oral history, or on our bodies. On 16 June 1976, 50 years ago, and in the days immediately after, between 600 and 1000 people were killed by the South African apartheid state. On 16 June 2026, we come together to

events.title: Turner & Constable
events.description: Two of Britain’s greatest painters, J.M.W. Turner and John Constable were also the greatest of rivals. Celebrating the 250th anniversary of their births, with Turner born in 1775 and Constable in 1776, this film explores their lives and legacies alongside a groundbreaking Tate Britain exhibition.

events.title: Sherman's March
events.description: Armed with a 16mm camera and a grant to make a documentary about Sherman’s 1864 march to the sea, Ross McElwee gets sidetracked, shifting his attention from the historical to the personal and the battlefield of modern love. This landmark of first-person filmmaking is newly re-released in a 4K restoration. Director Ross McElwee joins us for a post-screening conversation. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Volver
events.description: Equal parts tragedy, melodrama, and farce, this story of three generations of working-class Spanish women is a comedic and compassionate tribute to resilience in the face of life’s most outrageous tribulations. Penélope Cruz delivers an Oscar-nominated performance in Almodóvar’s modern masterpiece.

events.title: New international students arrive
events.address: 5 Lower Lake Road, South Hadley, MA
events.description: Check-in is all day beginning at 10 am. New international students arriving at campus will be met by upper-class international students and Orientation Leaders! Students will receive information about transportation to campus. Students should check in at Blanchard 205 and 227 where they will pick up keys, receive orientation materials, and much more.

events.title: Pre-orientation begins for new international students
events.description: All new international students are required to attend Pre-Orientation. Pre-Orientation concludes with dinner on Friday, when Orientation begins.

events.description: New students will move in between 8 am and noon. Details about move-in and Orientation will be sent to each student's MHC email address. Orientation begins after lunch.

events.title: A Body To Live In
events.description: A BODY TO LIVE IN offers an uncompromising look at the rise of BDSM performance art, body modification and the “modern primitives” cultural movement through the agonies and ecstasies of transgressive artist Fakir Musafar and the communities that surrounded him. Director Angelo Madsen joins us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Awake, My Soul: The Story of the Sacred Harp
events.description: Explore the history, music, and traditions of Sacred Harp singing, which has survived over 200 years tucked away from sight in the rural deep South. This haunting, moving music lives on today at community singing events. Post-film Q&A with filmmaker Matt Hinton.

events.title: First They Came for My College
events.description: When Florida Governor Ron DeSantis stages a coup at New College, the state’s beloved public honors college, students and professors confront a new reality: their campus is ground zero in an unprecedented nationwide assault on academic freedom and diversity. Director Patrick Bresnan and producer Harry William Hanbury join us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.
